risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Patients were assigned an allocation number and randomized into the study by an unblinded pharmacist using an Interactive Voice Response System." Comment: This describes a centralized IVRS-based randomization process, which is a proper random allocation method and is sufficient to judge the sequence generation as truly random.

Quote: "Patients were assigned an allocation number and randomized into the study by an unblinded pharmacist using an Interactive Voice Response System." Comment: An interactive voice response system is a central allocation mechanism that prevents the enrolling staff from foreseeing the next assignment, so allocation concealment is adequately described.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "This multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase-3 clinical trial" and "Patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to receive vernakalant or placebo." Comment: The study explicitly states it was double-blind and placebo-controlled, indicating participants and personnel were intended to be blinded to allocation. The outcomes reported here (conversion to sinus rhythm, symptoms, and adverse events) could in principle be influenced by knowledge of assignment, but the documented blinding makes differential performance bias unlikely.

Quote: "All ECGs, including 12-lead Holter monitoring, were adjudicated by blinded members of the CEC." and "Patients were confirmed to be in AF at baseline by independent CEC assessment of 12-lead ECG recordings." Comment: The key efficacy outcomes (conversion to sinus rhythm, time to conversion, ECG/Holter-derived events) were assessed by blinded independent committee members using ECG recordings, which are objective measurements and unlikely to be influenced by knowledge of allocation. Although one secondary outcome was patient-reported AF symptoms, the main outcome assessment described here was blinded and based on objective ECG adjudication, so detection bias is low for this domain.

Quote: "All randomized patients who received any amount of study drug were included in the efficacy and safety analyses." and "A total of 123 participants were randomized in a 1:1 ratio... of these, 111 (vernakalant: n = 55; placebo, n = 56) received any amount of study drug and were included in the efficacy and safety analyses." Comment: Attrition appears limited and balanced after randomization, with only 12/123 randomized participants not receiving study drug (6 per group). The paper indicates that all treated randomized participants were analyzed, and reasons for non-treatment are not reported as outcome-related. This makes bias from incomplete outcome data unlikely.
